steps.title: Identify what the function s(t) represents
steps.content:
The problem states that $s(t)$ gives the approximate mass of this isotope that remains after $t$ years.

Critically, this function outputs the amount still present, not the amount that has decayed.

This distinction immediately eliminates the other choices, which misinterpret the function output.

steps.title: Decode the equation s(62) = 36
steps.content:
The equation $s(62) = 36$ tells us:
  • Input: $t = 62$ years have elapsed
  • Output: $s(62) = 36$ grams remain
Since $s(t)$ represents remaining mass, this equation directly means that 36 grams of the sample remain 62 years after decay begins. A is correct.
